In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:妻科神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor rank)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} |Festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}, {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}, north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り}}. Like {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}, the consort deity of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}.[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}''' : Recognized as the consort of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:妻科神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor rank)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} |Festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}, {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}, north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り}}. Like {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}, the consort deity of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}.[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}''' : Recognized as the consort of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:妻科神社]]

Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:妻科神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor rank)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} |Festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}, {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}, north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り}}. Like {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}, the consort deity of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}.[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}''' : Recognized as the consort of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:妻科神社]]

With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:妻科神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor rank)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} |Festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}, {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}, north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り}}. Like {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}, the consort deity of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}.[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}''' : Recognized as the consort of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor rank)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} |Festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}, {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}, north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り}}. Like {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}, the consort deity of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}.[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}''' : Recognized as the consort of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

{{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor rank)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} |Festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}, {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}, north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り}}. Like {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}, the consort deity of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}.[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}''' : Recognized as the consort of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor rank)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} |Festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}, {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}, north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り}}. Like {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}, the consort deity of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}.[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}}''' : Recognized as the consort of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} for recovery before eventually settling in {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪}}. The shrine is first mentioned in records from {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby burial mounds, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted two koku of land by the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Nagano|ja|長野市}} Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ({{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]
