Like [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}, the consort deity of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Tiger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Monkey|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 申}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of [[:en:2011|2011]].[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|qq=}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}''' : Recognized as the consort of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Hikokamiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|qq=}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|qq=}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the fifth year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Plain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長野盆地}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] for recovery before eventually settling in [[:en:Suwa|Suwa]]. The shrine is first mentioned in records from [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby Kofuns, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was granted two koku of land by the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]]. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gururin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ぐるりん}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano District Court|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|qq=}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidome Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|妻科神社|version=101016536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]] [[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |address = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank)
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]], [[:en:October 2|October 2]] |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]], north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Office Street|qq=}}. Like [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}, the consort deity of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Tiger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Monkey|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 申}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of [[:en:2011|2011]].[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|qq=}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}''' : Recognized as the consort of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|qq=}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|qq=}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the fifth year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Plain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長野盆地}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] for recovery before eventually settling in [[:en:Suwa|Suwa]]. The shrine is first mentioned in records from [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby Kofuns, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was granted two koku of land by the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]]. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gururin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ぐるりん}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano District Court|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|qq=}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidome Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|妻科神社|version=101016536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]] [[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447213]] Wikidata name: Tsumashina Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |address = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank)
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]], [[:en:October 2|October 2]] |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]], north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Office Street|qq=}}. Like [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}, the consort deity of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Tiger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Monkey|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 申}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of [[:en:2011|2011]].[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|qq=}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}''' : Recognized as the consort of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|qq=}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|qq=}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the fifth year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Plain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長野盆地}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] for recovery before eventually settling in [[:en:Suwa|Suwa]]. The shrine is first mentioned in records from [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby Kofuns, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was granted two koku of land by the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]]. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gururin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ぐるりん}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano District Court|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|qq=}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidome Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|妻科神社|version=101016536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]] [[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447213]] Wikidata name: Tsumashina Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall |address = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|39|13.46|N|138|10|44.77|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuma Shina Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank)
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]], [[:en:October 2|October 2]] |Rituals = }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]], north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Office Street|qq=}}. Like [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}, the consort deity of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Tiger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Monkey|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 申}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of [[:en:2011|2011]].[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|qq=}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}''' : Recognized as the consort of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|qq=}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|qq=}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the fifth year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Plain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長野盆地}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] for recovery before eventually settling in [[:en:Suwa|Suwa]]. The shrine is first mentioned in records from [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby Kofuns, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was granted two koku of land by the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]]. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gururin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ぐるりん}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano District Court|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|qq=}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidome Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|妻科神社|version=101016536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]] [[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447213]] Wikidata name: Tsumashina Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG |address = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.653739|N|138.179103|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome|qid=Q9150392}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank)
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]], [[:en:October 2|October 2]] |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Minami-Nagano|qid=Q11408697}} }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]], north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Office Street|qq=}}. Like [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}, the consort deity of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Tiger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Monkey|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 申}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of [[:en:2011|2011]].[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|qq=}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}''' : Recognized as the consort of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|qq=}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|qq=}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the fifth year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Plain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長野盆地}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] for recovery before eventually settling in [[:en:Suwa|Suwa]]. The shrine is first mentioned in records from [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby Kofuns, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was granted two koku of land by the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]]. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
With onbashira (sacred pillars) on both sides. ==Auxiliary Shrines== * Tenjin Shrine * Shotoku Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Sericulture Shrine ==Practical Information== ;Address * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 ;Access * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the {{ill|Gururin Bus|ja|ぐるりん号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gururin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ぐるりん}} (C route) and alight at "Giin Kaikan-mae" bus stop. ;Nearby Landmarks * {{ill|Nagano District Court|ja|長野地方裁判所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano District Court|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}} * Hotel Kokusai 21 * {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * "Nagano Prefecture Place names" ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) * Keichirō Kobayashi, “Tsuma Shina Shrine” in {{ill|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|ja|日本の神々|1=Kamigami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (edited by {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|qq=}}, published by {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=}}). ==See Also== ;Other {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} - One of the Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidome Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidome Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=}} ==External Links== * [http://zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-182.html Tsuma Shina Shrine] (Zenkoji Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|妻科神社|version=101016536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsumashina Shrine]] [[ja:妻科神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447213]] Wikidata name: Tsumashina Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsuma Shina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:妻科神社 拝殿.JPG |address = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] Ōaza Minami-Nagano, Aza Hongo 219-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.653739|N|138.179103|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yasakatome|qid=Q9150392}} |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank)
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Unknown |MainBuilding = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Alternatename = |PilgrimageStations = One of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]], [[:en:October 2|October 2]] |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Minami-Nagano|qid=Q11408697}} }} '''Tsuma Shina Shrine''' (妻科神社, Tsuma Shina Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. It is one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and part of the {{ill|Zenkoji Seven Shrines|ja|善光寺七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Seven Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ==Overview== [[File:妻科神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located southwest of [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]], north of the {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Government Office|ja|長野県庁舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Government Office|qq=}}, in a residential area off {{ill|Prefectural Office Street|ja|県庁通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Office Street|qq=}}. Like [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], the shrine enshrines {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}, the consort deity of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}} are also Suwa-related shrines. These four shrines host the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] during the {{ill|Year of the Tiger|ja|寅年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Tiger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅}} and {{ill|Year of the Monkey|ja|申年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of the Monkey|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 申}}, with onbashira (sacred pillars) erected in their precincts. The area south of the shrine is notable for having the highest residential land value in Nagano Prefecture for 12 consecutive years as of [[:en:2011|2011]].[http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=20851 Nagano land prices - All locations continue to fall] ({{ill|Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|ja|長野日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Nichinichi Shimbun|qq=}}, March 16, 2011). ==deity== ===Main deity=== * '''{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}}''' : Recognized as the consort of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]. Some believe the enshrined deity is their child, {{ill|Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|ja|妻科比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shina Hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ===Associated deity=== * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} There is a theory suggesting these two associated deities represent the misinterpretation of {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|ja|建御名方富命彦神別神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as two separate entities.‘Tsuma Shina Shrine’ in "Nagano Prefecture Place names". Another interpretation identifies {{ill|Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|彦神別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as {{ill|Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Hikokamuwake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}.See “{{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社#deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine#Deities|qq=}}”. ==History== ===Foundation=== The founding date is unknown. The name "Tsuma Shina" suggests that the shrine serves as the consort shrine to the great shrine of {{ill|Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenminakafutomi no Mikoto Hikokamuwake Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|qq=}}. Some associate these shrines with the "Minochi Shrine" mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the fifth year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign (691 CE). According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], after being defeated by {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, fled north from Izumo along the coastline, eventually reaching the {{ill|Zenkoji Plain|ja|善光寺平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Plain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長野盆地}} near modern-day Zenkoji. His consort hid in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Susobana River|ja|裾花川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susobana River|qq=}} to escape the chaos, which led to the naming of this site. Takeminakata later fled to [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] for recovery before eventually settling in [[:en:Suwa|Suwa]]. The shrine is first mentioned in records from [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami," suggesting it was originally a local land deity. The shrine's historical roots may also lie in ancient rituals associated with nearby Kofuns, as evidenced by the local name "Treasure Mound Street." Obsidian arrowheads have also been discovered in the area. ===Later History=== The shrine is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as "Tsuma Shina Shrine" in Minochi District, Shinano Province, granting it {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} status. It has been considered one of the {{ill|Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|ja|善光寺三鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Three Guardian Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} alongside {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was granted two koku of land by the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]]. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was elevated to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} status. ===Shinto Rankings=== * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 2 (860 CE), elevated from {{ill|Jushii-ge|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} to {{ill|Jugoi-ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) - Listed as "Tsuma Shina Ji-no-Kami" * Jōgan 5 (863 CE), elevated to {{ill|Jugoi-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) ==Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall - Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], located on a terrace behind the worship hall. * Worship Hall * Norito Hall * Shrine Kitchen * Shrine Office File:妻科神社 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:妻科神社 拝殿正面.JPG|Worship Hall
